2603.23822.title: How Vulnerable Are Edge LLMs?
2603.23822.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed on edge devices under strict computation and quantization constraints, yet their security implications remain unclear. We study query-based knowledge extraction from quantized edge-deployed LLMs under realistic query budgets and show that, although quantization introduces noise, it does not remove the underlying semantic knowledge, allowing substantial behavioral recovery through carefully designed queries. To systematically analyze this risk, we propose \textbf{CLIQ} (\textbf{Cl}ustered \textbf{I}nstruction \textbf{Q}uerying), a structured query construction framework that improves semantic coverage while reducing redundancy. Experiments on quantized Qwen models (INT8/INT4) demonstrate that CLIQ consistently outperforms original queries across BERTScore, BLEU, and ROUGE, enabling more efficient extraction under limited budgets. These results indicate that quantization alone does not provide effective protection against query-based extraction, highlighting a previously underexplored security risk in edge-deployed LLMs.
2603.23822.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地在邊緣設備上部署，面臨嚴格的計算和量化限制，但其安全性影響仍不明確。<lb/>我們研究了在現實查詢預算下，從量化的邊緣部署LLMs中基於查詢的知識提取，並顯示儘管量化引入了噪音，但並未消除潛在的語義知識，通過精心設計的查詢可以實現實質性的行為恢復。<lb/>為了系統性地分析這一風險，我們提出了\textbf{CLIQ}（\textbf{Cl}ustered \textbf{I}nstruction \textbf{Q}uerying），這是一個結構化的查詢構建框架，旨在提高語義覆蓋率，同時減少冗餘。<lb/>對量化Qwen模型（INT8/INT4）的實驗表明，CLIQ在BERTScore、BLEU和ROUGE指標上始終優於原始查詢，使得在有限預算下的提取更加高效。<lb/>這些結果表明，僅僅依靠量化並不能有效防止基於查詢的提取，突顯了邊緣部署LLMs中一個先前未被充分探索的安全風險。
2603.23822.author: Ao Ding et.al.
2603.23822.authors: Ao Ding, Hongzong Li, Zi Liang, Zhanpeng Shi, Shuxin Zhuang, Shiqin Tang, Rong Feng, Ping Lu
